What do you say Eta?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Less cameras to avoid at his place than the hotels" she<br />shrugged,” Better plan than any". <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">They wrapped up their dinner, and Chester waived to Albert, and the three<br />headed to his place. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Vision<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Eta and Chester<br />go to Chesters' house; and she has another dream. This dream is of a bomb at<br />the parade. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Chester is giving them the grand tour. “Kitchen is through the left there,<br />there is a Kurieg there, if you drink coffee. There's also a latte wand if you<br />are the frills type coffee drinker.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash is following just behind her, she can feel his warmth. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Nope, not going there. Get a grip girl.” She said under her breath.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Chester pointed to a big room just off the landing, “The movie theater is<br />set up with a touch screen display, just scroll through the movie you want to<br />see.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Wait…” Eta said, “Did you say movie theater?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Yes I did.” Chester shrugged, “If you close the doors you can turn the<br />volume up as loud as you want it. It's totally sound proof from the rest of the<br />house." He smiled big about that one. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Some house you got here Chester.” Ash threw a glance at her, eyebrows<br />raised.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Thanks, follow me.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Heading up the stairs, then down the hall to the right, they passed several<br />doors. Pointing to Ash, "You can take the first door on the left, and Eta,<br />you can take the next one. Just so you know, my working hours are just<br />starting, so I'll most likely be asleep when you two wake up. The house is<br />yours. I’m usually up about mid-morning to noon. If you need a computer there<br />is a guest computer in the living room. I'll let you get settled, I'll be in my<br />office on the third floor if anyone needs me." He nodded, and headed up<br />the second flight of stairs.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Who is this guy?" Eta said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I don't know… but I think we signed up for the wrong career."<br />Ash said. "I'll see you in the morning". <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta nodded, and then headed to her room. Suite, was more like it. It had<br />its own bathtub with jets, and empty walk-in closet. She put her bag on the<br />chaise, went to bed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Suddenly, she was not in the room anymore. She was floating above the<br />house. The wind was whipping through her. She wanted to see closer to the<br />ground, and found her self floating toward the directino her eye had been<br />looking. At ground level, the light was still on in the house. Chester was<br />working on something. She thought of Ash, and found herself floating through<br />the windows, doors, walls, and into his room. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash was sitting on a chair, by the window. His hand under his chin, as he<br />stared outside. He seemed to be lost in thought. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">An image filled her mind. A tower. Then she was whisked away from the<br />house, and went flying through the air. She was passing over houses at sonic<br />speads. No need to put her arms out like superwoman, she was just along for the<br />ride. As her flight slowed, she came upon a gated compound. There was a<br />manicured entrance, and private secruity. Multiple buildings on the property,<br />but the most interesting was a large tower, 13 stories tall. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">At the top of the tower, a light was on through a corner window. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta floated to the top of the building, and saw a man with white hair,<br />typing into a computer. He turned and looked in her direction, but he didn't<br />see her. He just stared outside while he spoke into his bluetooth headset. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Floating down, and then backward, she was hurled through the night, back<br />into her room. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">It's getting worse... these visions... she needs answers. But this one, it<br />was different. It was real, raw.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Glancing at the clock, it's 11:02 am. She over slept. I guess overslept is<br />an understatement when you have no plans, but she felt like it was over<br />sleeping. She dressed in a hurry and worked her way down the hall. The smell of<br />food carried her down the stairs and around the corner. Ash was flipping<br />pancakes and stirring sausage. Chester was sitting at the counter talking… then<br />he turned around to see Eta walk in. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Hey. I'm not normally awake this early, but Ash discovered cookware<br />in my kitchen, and I followed the scent down stairs. I didn't even know that<br />stuff was in there!" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I'm glad he found it, I'm starving." Eta gave a nod to Chester,<br />and sat down at one of the bar stools. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash turned to her, "You ok Slugger? I heard you tossing and turning a<br />bit last night. It was fitful enough I could hear it from next door." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Yeah, I'm alright… but…"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash turned around. "What is it?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I had another one." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Really? What about this time?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta glanced at Chester and hesitated.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"It's ok…I trust him. I had a buddy run a background on our friend<br />here. No offense Chester." Chester smiled and waived, mouth full of<br />coffee, "And it turns out that Chester's security clearance is almost as<br />high as ours"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta gave Ash a glance. "OK, what do you do? Exactly, I mean?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"A little of this, a little of that." Chester laughed.<br />"Here's the thing." Chester pulls out his business card and hands it<br />to Eta. "Internet Data and Program Security Analyst - Chester Jandihar<br />IDAPSA".<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Wow", she says, handing it to Ash.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Yeah, it's a mouthful. But CEO's get all freaked out when you call<br />yourself a Hacker, so the business title makes them feel better. Basically, I<br />design programs of various kinds for businesses, non-profits, even the<br />government. I also test systems for vulnerabilities and report my findings.<br />Then in my free time I just build random stuff, programs mostly." Chester<br />shrugged, "So as I said, a little of this, and little of that." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash smiles, "He's consulted for the N.S.A."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta raised an eyebrow in disbelief. "If you say so…" Eta began to<br />recant the visions she'd been having to catch Chester up to speed. Then the<br />most recent vision, detail for detail, as best as she could remember it. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"You must think we're both nuts… huh?" Eta turned to Chester. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Actually." Chester mused, "I find this fascinating. We've<br />been studying this kind of phenomenon for years." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"You have?" Eta turned to give her full attention to Chester now.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Chester pulled up his tablet, and starting scrolling through files.<br />"I've worked on a lot of wicked crazy projects, but some of them have been<br />about the kinds of experiences you two are talking about. This one…" Chester<br />handed the tablet to Eta who scrolled through the site. "… for example.<br />The Esion Eti'hw Institute &amp; Research Facility. They pronounce that second<br />word like Et-Eew. I built some seriously intense stuff for them. They're just<br />the kind of people who dabble in your experiences. I wouldn't use them though,<br />they give me the heebeegeebees. Especially that main guy… what's his<br />name.Thaddeus. I finally turned the project over to some eager MIT brats. I<br />couldn't work with them anymore."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"What kinds of projects? What's the research-" Eta took a quick<br />breath. "The tower." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Yeah," Chester pointed to the picture, "that's their pride<br />and joy there. Esion Tower." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"No, you don't understand, my vision, I was at that tower last<br />night." She recounted her vision to them.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"The white haired man had to be this guy" Chester took the<br />tablet, navigated to the about page, and handed it back. "Thaddeus J. B.<br />Rotcudnoc, say that ten times fast." he laughed.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta pulled up the About page and read the first line. "Have you ever<br />wondered what is just beyond this world? Have you thought there had to be more?<br />Religion and Spiritualists have let you down? Turn to science. Our work in<br />quantam mechanics and extra deminsional evolution, is providing solid scienctific<br />answers to many of life's olderst questions. The Esion Eti'hw Institute was<br />founded on the ground breaking principals outlined in the thesis 'Unlocking the<br />Extra-Normal Mind. A study of the human mind’s unlocked potential', By Dr.<br />Richard T. Briar." Sounds freaky, just like the rest of the kooks I've<br />been reading about the last few weeks. "<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Wait… did you say Dr. Briar? Let me see that." Ash grabbed the<br />tablet out her hand. He scrolled to the bottom and stopped. His brow furrowed,<br />and lips pursed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"What's the problem? You look like you've been punched." Eta<br />observed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"The night before the attack, this guy sat across from me at the<br />coffee shop. He was asking a bunch of weird questions about the encounter with<br />the homeless guy. It was almost as if he knew I had seen something, but I just<br />passed it off as though it was nothing. I had forgotten about him until just<br />now."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"So, you have strange encounter where you see something that wasn't<br />there, theoretically…" Chester interjected, "… then this guy shows up<br />asking questions. Then that night you are attacked by assassins. Yep, I knew<br />that place gave me the creeps." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta could feel her skin tighten, and the hair on the back of her neck stand<br />up. "So what does this mean?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I have no idea, but I need to get some answers. I'm going to do some<br />field reconniasance. You two be alright for a few hours?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Sure," Eta said. "I'll clean my guns. I have a feeling I'll<br />be needing them." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Cool! You can set that up here on the counter. I'll start checking<br />out what I can about this institute, see what they've been up to since I<br />stopped working with them. See? I knew I was here for a reason." Chester<br />grinned. He was cute kid.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Ash's reaction to<br />meeting Chester<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Ash<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">He heads out to scout<br />out the towers, and do some field work.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash drove<br />the Vulcan to a military surplus store, and restocked a few items he thought he<br />would need. He picked up two uniforms to match the secuirty in the website<br />photos. Then he turned the bike north onto the I-10, and headed to Santa<br />Monica. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">On the way,<br />Ash could not get the vision of the large man behind Chester out of his mind.<br />The things he often saw on people, the medication he'd been taking for<br />halucinations. Now it turns out he might not be crazy after all? Or maybe he<br />still was. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">***** Ash<br />needs to have a sighting here, and it needs to urge him on.*****<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Orphanage<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Eta tags along to<br />an Orphanage, where a child prophecies her future, and confirms the bomb is<br />real.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I<br />have an appointment to make, and this program will take a few hours to<br />run." Chester took his last sip of Mountain Dew, and threw the bottle in<br />the trash can. "You are welcome to stay here and get some rest. Or, you<br />can come along if you'd like."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta glanced<br />at the code running in the background. "Sure" With a nod, she left a<br />note for Ash, and they grabbed their coats and headed out the door. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">In the car<br />Chester rambled on and on about this Orphanage they were going to. "Ms. Wu<br />is one of the most inspiring people I've ever met. She was raised in mainland<br />China. She was a second child, but her parents refused to abort her, so she<br />lived without honor or privilege. Eventually, when she was 15, he parents sent<br />her to the USA hoping for a better life. She was mistreated by her "tour<br />guides", but eventually broke free. Now she runs an Orphanage here in<br />L.A." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester<br />nodded to the building. It was a nice place for being an Orphanage. It looked<br />more like a private university campus. "She takes the ones that no one<br />else will take. Many of her kids will spend the rest of their childhoods here,<br />some the rest of their lives."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">They<br />parked, and walked in. The entrance was beautiful, laid with quality white<br />tile, and a marble receptionist’s desk. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Hey<br />Ellaine", Chester waived to the young lady, who couldn't have been more<br />than 17. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">She nodded,<br />and smiled, but then turned back to her telephone. "Yes sir, and what is<br />the address?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester led<br />her to the common room. A library ran the length of one wall, with seating<br />areas to read. There were cubicle style desks with computers, and a separate<br />area room to the side for games. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">A woman<br />walked in from an adjacent office, "Peter." she said to a young man<br />with a hoodie. "Did you complete your homework before turning on the<br />game?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes<br />Ms. Wu." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Show<br />me..."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Um...<br />" the kid fidgeted, "I'll go get that started right now Ms. Wu."<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Good<br />boy." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester<br />pointed to the boy as he left, "That kid has been through 14 foster homes,<br />and he's 16. There is almost no chance he could be adopted anywhere now. But<br />Ms. Wu keeps him in line, and he's been showing great improvement, he just<br />needed to have someone show him love and discipline." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"How<br />did you find this place?" Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Well..."<br />Chester smiled, "I was given a list of places I could use to fulfill<br />community service, and I threw darts at the wall. The first one stuck on this<br />place. What I didn't know, was how much Ms. Wu would mean to me. She treated me<br />like one of her troubled kids, and she was just as tough on me as them." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Ms.<br />Wu, Good to see you." Chester said as the woman approached. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">She was<br />small, maybe 4'9"? It was hard to say, but she looked like she was in her<br />late 60's. After some introductions, Ms. Wu turned to Eta, "Dear, I need<br />this young man for a few minutes to fix my computers. These kids keep downloading<br />things. Would you be willing to wait here?" Eta nodded in the affirmative.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta walked<br />over to the book shelves and glanced at the titles. They seemed to have<br />everything from Honors World History to Sesame Street. Eta grabbed a book on US<br />History that had pictures, and sat down in a nearby chair. She put her feet up<br />on the ottoman, and leafed through the pictures.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">After a few<br />minutes, a young girl walked by to the book shelf and sat down next to her.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Your<br />pretty" she said, not looking up from her book. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Her speech<br />was odd. Not down syndrome, but maybe Autism, Eta thought. "Thank<br />you" Eta replied. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />have dreams..." The kid said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">A little<br />taken back, "Yes, most people do. Do you have dreams?" trying to make<br />conversation. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes..."<br />not looking up from her book, "... but not like yours. Yours come<br />true." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta was not<br />sure how to respond, she just stared at the girl for what felt like an<br />eternity. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />need to stop him. That tower scary.” The girl stood up, and walked away with<br />her book. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester walked<br />up behind her, “She another one that cannot be adopted.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Plan<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Chester gets into<br />the institute, and discovers the next key for Ash and Eta to solve. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Notes: suddenly, she<br />was not in the room anymore. She was floating again. Above a crowd. Hundreds of<br />people below. Large balloons in familiar shapes were passing by. On her far<br />right, Santa Clause was coming down the road in a fake sleigh with plastic reindeer.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Back at the house,<br /></span><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Chester was working on one side of the<br />counter, and Eta sat on the other side.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"You know the problem with these MIT brats?" Chester smirked.<br />"They are way too sure of themselves. They are so convinced that they're<br />the smartest kid in the room, and everyone has to answer to them. They've never<br />had to make it on their own, or do their own work. They get sloppy in their<br />life, and sloppy in their code. And that's how I got in." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Chester… if you don't mind me asking…" Eta kept her head down<br />clearing the barrel of her Barretta 9mm. "How does someone with your<br />obvious talent end up teaching computers for dummies at a homeless shelter, and<br />hanging out with orphans? <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Well, it's a long story, but not I'm not sure it's a terribly<br />interesting one... I was one of the youngest graduates of Caltech to date, I<br />was working with a major IT firm all bright eyed, ready to change the world.<br />Then I started to realize that all I was doing was making really ugly people a<br />lot of money. So I started drinking, then came the drugs.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Soon I couldn't code anymore so I lost my job. My parents couldn't bare the<br />fact their son turned into an addict, so they stopped returning my telephone<br />calls. I ended up doing things I'd rather forget."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Chester paused for a sip of coffee… "One day, standing in front of a<br />judge I was told I could take jail or A.A plus community service. So, I chose<br />A.A. and service. I had nowhere to go, but a guy at one of the meetings told me<br />about the Union Gospel Mission. So I stayed there… every night for months.<br />After a while I started to notice how badly the place needed new computers, but<br />couldn't budget them in. I made a few calls to an old professor I knew at<br />Caltech, and he sent me all the parts I needed to build new computers, custom<br />made for the shelter. They had never seen so many computers in one place, and<br />so high end. They were afraid they'd get stolen. I showed them how to lock them<br />down, and use them. Eventually I migrated from resident to volunteer, and<br />eventually honorary staff." &nbsp;I<br />create code and do odd projects for money, and I make enough in my spare time<br />to live better than most people, so I spend most of my time helping people down<br />at the mission. Consulting for the N.S.A. gave me a big break back into the<br />world of coding professionally. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">It's not the life my parents had in mind… but it's more satisfying than<br />making rich people a lot of money so that they can spend it on their mistresses<br />and toys. Pardon me a moment, this part will take some concentration…"<br />Chester's' fingers where flying again. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">It's amazing how fast the kids fingers could type. This kid could type in<br />his coding language, faster that Eta could think in English. He was something<br />else. She called him “kid”, but he wasn’t that much younger than her. Not bad<br />on the eyes either.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">As he was distracted with code, she wandered the room studying his life.<br />You can never turn off the solider, or the spy. Besides, even pointless<br />analysis of the contents of his bookshelf was better than the thoughts of what<br />might be happening to… No. She's not allowing herself to think that way. She<br />must stay focused. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Even if that means being focused on "Quantum Mechanics for<br />Mathematicians by Leon A. Takhtajan". Really? Who is this guy?! Eta looked<br />out the window and watched some birds pecking in the yard…<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Hey, this is interesting." Chester waived her over.<br />"xxxxxxxxxxx<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">*****hatching a plan to break into the tower. And bug the<br />CEO's office and computer.*****<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Tower<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Ash and Eta break<br />into the intitute to get informatino out of their computer, then they are<br />discovered. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Hopefully the intel Chester gave them was still current. The compound<br />didn't seem heavily guarded, Ash knew that missing personnel would just trigger<br />the alarm. He snuck in through a side window after the guards had passed. He<br />could see men in lab coats running tests and reading outputs. There was the<br />slight scent of rubbing alcohol, lemon scented. Ash opened the door of an<br />unused room, and got out of the hallway, out of sight. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta followed closely behind a few moments later. She looked tired. She’s<br />not sleeping well, he thought to himself. Moving their way across the room to<br />another doorway on the opposite side of the large room, a slight opening<br />allowed enough sightline to assess the situation on the other side. They would<br />need to be quick. Ash and Eta moved silently and swiftly down the hall toward<br />the office towers. They were dark, so odds were that they were under light<br />guard and they could be in and out without being seen.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The elevator would be easier, but that would alert someone to its use. The<br />stairs would be longer, but they would have a greater chance of avoiding their<br />being spotted. Hopefully the cameras were already looped, they'd have to rely<br />on Chester to complete his part of the mission. They didn't want to risk<br />breaking radio silence to confirm. If he saw them heading into a trap, he'd<br />break silence to alert them.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">As they entered the main floor of the office suite they checked infrared<br />and found no motion detectors in the immediate area. Chester advised heading<br />for the admins computer, the password security of an admin is often overlooked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">They inserted the thumb drive provided by Chester, and turned on the<br />computer. It whirred to life, and started booting. Chester's program hacked in<br />through the boot drive into the main file system and onto the servers. Once<br />uploaded they could leave, because the program would begin uploading all the<br />sensitive data to multiple safe servers around the country, and back up servers<br />behind those. These people may or may not be sophisticated enough to track all<br />that down, but not until Chester had a way to recover the files from one of the<br />backup points, without leaving a trace behind.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The upload of the info-virus was complete. Eta and Ash turned off the<br />monitor but left the computer running. Chester had advised that the virus would<br />shut down the computer before anyone came in for the day, after it was done<br />with it’s work.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash took the thumb drive and stepped on it to crack the case open. He took<br />a screw driver and punctured the memory chip with a hard punch, then repeated<br />this a few times. He then handed it to Eta who placed it onto a super magnet<br />she was carrying in her pocket. This should sufficiently erase the data. And if<br />by some miracle they are able to recover the data enough to understand the<br />encryption for the virus, it will be long after Chester has recovered what he<br />needed from the server network he used to store the mined data.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Making their way back down the stairs, footsteps and chatter below gave<br />them pause. Ten levels down. The guards were making their rounds, chatting<br />about the latest football game. Ash looked up, and didn't see anyone that<br />direction. It would be a tight race, but it was the plan B they had discussed.<br />They kept the foot falls as silent as possible as they made their way back up<br />the stairs, trying to keep an ear on the guards below, and not alert them to<br />their presence.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">A few floors up, and they heard the sound of radio chatter. They stopped to<br />listen. The guard room couldn't see the guards in the stair well. The loop had<br />been discovered, they were compromised, but no-one knew they were here, they<br />heard the guards coming up the stairs faster now. Ash and Eta kept to the walls<br />and made their way quickly. If they stepped light enough the guards wouldn't<br />hear them over their incessant chatter and yelling "Clear".<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash and Eta were so focused on the guards below, that they failed to watch<br />the doorways as they passed them. The door opened just as they passed it, and<br />lights flashed from the rifle.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Or was that a rifle?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">It was a bright blue light... not the yellow red muzzle flash it should<br />have been.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash knew it as he was going down...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">...he'd been tazed.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">He'd known that feeling from years of training. They used to taz each other<br />just for fun, and built up a tolerance to it. In a one on one fight, Ash could<br />have come back fighting, but a then second taz hit him. The guards from below<br />had caught up.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Three, no four men were on him, holding him down. The needle pinching his<br />arm would mean powerful sleep. These were professionals. Now he knew what it<br />felt like to be the victim of a smash and grab. Such a rookie mistake too,<br />letting his guard down. They should have come in slower, or divided their<br />attention.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">More surveillance.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">More reconnaissance.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">As he felt himself going under the power of sedation, he forced his eyes<br />slightly open, he could see a figure coming toward him dressed in black. He<br />closed his eyes, but he heard a sigh. It sounded like disappointment.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Was that himself sighing?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Darkness came over him...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">... The room always started out so bright. The white room. White walls,<br />cold metal, hissing from overhead... the familiar dream, the dream he'd had<br />every night for five years now... The dark room would be next. He hated the<br />dark room. But the white room wasn't so bad, except for that incessant hissing.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Escape<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Eta escapes the<br />institute, and makes it back to Chester's place. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta ran for all her legs would carry her, three guards were behind her. She<br />was losing them faster, she could outrun these mall cops any day. Up ahead she<br />saw the door open, and one came toward her. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Running time was over for the moment. She'd have to stand her ground<br />against this one, and the three behind. She saw the Taser flash, she side<br />swiped and it made no contact. She used the momentum to grab the Taser and pull<br />it toward her, while she pulled in her knee to his inner thigh. Whipping around<br />she tossed him like a rag doll at the other three coming up the stairs.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">In another life, she could have just shot them all and been done with it,<br />but these were innocent guards. She broke into their facility. These were not<br />enemy combatants, and she couldn’t just blow them all away. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Still, getting caught isn’t an option either.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">For split fractions of a second time slowed down. Should she stand against<br />these four or had she thrown them off enough to regain her lead? Nope. One had<br />gotten passed the others without losing momentum.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">He came at her with the Taser too. What is it with these guys and Tasers?<br />She allowed the wires to fly passed as she used the stairwell handle for grip<br />and plucked herself up into the air, and landed a kick right to the face. He<br />hurled back into the other three who were just getting untangled. That was the<br />break in momentum she needed.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta flew up the stairs faster now, highest speed. She couldn't worry about<br />Ash right now. He could handle himself. If he'd been caught, she couldn't help<br />him if she were caught too. Up the stairs, out to the access door to the roof.<br />She pulled her side arm and fired three rounds into the padlock and landed a<br />good jump kick into the door, which flew open before her. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">She tore a piece of her jacket and let it hang on the emergency exit<br />latter. She then threw it down the side of the building. It carried enough<br />weight with her extra gear that caught the wind, and landed out toward the edge<br />of the field. They would hunt the fake her down in the fields.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Meanwhile, she found an access panel, and hid inside the ducting. She<br />waited for a half hour to ensure that they were all on the hunt for her<br />elsewhere, and then Eta slowly exited the access panel. Eta worked her way down<br />the fire escape on the dark side of the building. She could see they'd taken<br />the ruse. Whole rounds of guards with flash lights were working their way through<br />the field, other in vehicles were driving the field looking for farther signs<br />of escape. There were even two helicopters searching the fields further out.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta noticed that two of the guards on break had left their flak jackets<br />hanging on the stairwell when they ran out to join the chase. Fortuitous! She<br />donned the jack that matched the other guards, the pants were so similar they'd<br />never have been inspected close up. She took the tie back from her hair, and<br />let it down with a shake. She walked in a hurried manner, but purposed to look<br />like someone on a mission for the boss (not someone running for her life)<br />toward the gate.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">She slipped out the back gate right toward all the fuss, and took a hummer<br />with her, why did they always leave the keys in these things? She picked up a<br />guard along the way, and ordered him to watch from the passenger side and she<br />looked out from the driver’s side. They whirred along, he tried to talk once,<br />and she reprimanded him for not paying attention. "We have a fugitive on<br />the loose and you want to make small talk soldier?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"No Ma'am!” came his reply. He didn't so much as look her direction<br />for the rest of the journey. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">She stopped along the road and told him she thought she saw something and<br />told him to head over the hill and look, if he spotted anything to call it in.<br />Meanwhile, she'd check the other side of the road. She was so bossy and<br />official that he never second guessed her and went running up and over the hill.<br />She took off down the road and toward the town.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Eta pulled in behind a truck stop, lost the flack jacket and ripped off the<br />bottom of ther cargo pants, and twisted the belly of her under shirt into a<br />knot. Nothing would attract attention away from her face faster than this get<br />up. She headed through the area toward the sleepers. Found an older Ford Escort<br />parked on the darker side of the building, late 1990's. She pulled out her<br />Leatherman and worked on hotwiring. In two minutes she was driving up the on<br />ramp toward the I-10.
