She need to break radio silence, but not yet, not until<br />she was sufficiently out of range.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Debate<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Dr. Briar sits at the monitor, watching as they place Ash in the chair, and<br />cuff him in place. Similar setting to when he first met him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Thaddeus, we cannot push him any further, he's already been in the room<br />longer than most others, and his vitals are dangerously out of control. We must<br />put an end to this test… or give him a rest and start again. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"You Dr. Briar, like these experiments, are temporary and<br />expendable…&nbsp; you would do well to<br />remember that."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">A chill crept over his spine, from his tail bone, up his neck, and into his<br />head.. His skin became cold as the realization dawned on him… He was never the<br />one in control. When he'd brought Thaddeus on, he'd been so hungry for the<br />financial backing and the seeming eagerness that he showed in the project, he'd<br />never stopped to consider that Thaddeus would take over. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"…I'll be in the observation lounge that I paid so much money to<br />build. If we lose him, I will handle it. Proceed." Thaddeus stepped out of<br />the room, and walked down the hall.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Dr. Briar slumped into his chair with the realization… he'd sold his<br />science, his soul, to this partnership. Without this financial backing, he'd<br />have to pull out and stop everything. Shameful though it may be, the scientist<br />in him was curious to see what would happen if he pushed the patient over the<br />age. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">So he turned back to the monitor, and kept one eye on the vitals, then<br />turned pushed the slider one notch higher. "It's beginning... the<br />biometrics just spiked."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Good." The answer was curt. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The previous test subjects had nowhere near the discipline that Ash did.<br />This should be an interesting observation. He''d been incredible so far.<br />Getting a trained military man, accustomed to being alone for days and weeks, a<br />survival expert. He had already outlasted every scientist, street volunteer,<br />and audio geek they had ran through the tests. Many of them didn't make it<br />through the prep room, but they never had anyone last this long, he'd been in<br />there for three hours and just now he's starting to show signs of a dimensional<br />tear.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"You are a genius!", Dr. Brair spoke into the observation lounge<br />microphone.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Yes, Dr. Briar I am! That is why you brought me here." Thaddeus<br />replied. "To fix this reject project and bring something valuable out of<br />it. Keep me appraised of any changes, I'm leaving for now." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Dr. Briar could see through the window as the black suited man turned and<br />left the room. His tuft of white hair bouncing along as he walked away.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">******<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">That was 8 years ago, the begining of the end of his life. Now he was still<br />here, cleaning up the mess his Benefactor had left behind. Ash was stirring, he<br />would go and complete the mission. His stomach turned at the thought.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">******** Run some tests on him while he's out.********<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Trapped<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Ash is trapped in<br />the institute, and is confronted by Dr. Briar. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The silence<br />was deafening. Except for that constant hiss from above. The light was still<br />too bright to open his eyes. But the sound was familiar. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">I know this<br />place...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The dream<br />again. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He could<br />feel cold steel against his wrists. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">This was<br />new. He didn't recall being locked to a chair in his dream. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Then he<br />heard... click. click. click. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">A clock?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash forced<br />his eyes open to see a room very similar to one from his dream. As his eyes<br />focused, he could see that this one was longer, the table was at one end. And<br />there was a single clock on the wall. This room had a pronounced and obvious<br />door, with a glass window.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Oh<br />good... you are awake. I'll be with you in just a little while."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">That voice.<br />I know that voice.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash took in<br />his surroundings. Nothing much to see. Nothing but white walls, white chair,<br />white table, white clock. Silver Handcuffs on his wrists. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">No silly<br />outfit, he was simply wearing his black gear from the night before, minus the<br />weapons. Blood had dried on his sleeve where the guard got a good kick with his<br />boot. It looked like they patched him up, maybe a few stitches in the shoulder.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash noticed<br />four cameras, one in each corner. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The door<br />opened, and in walked a small bald man in a lab coat. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Dr.<br />Briar."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Good<br />morning Col. Ashland. It's nice to have you back among us. You took a pretty<br />nasty fall last night, and put three of my guards in the hospital."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Glad<br />I could help." Ash Smiled.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Always make<br />sure your captor knows you are in charge. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar<br />returned the smile. "It seems you and I have some unfinished business. As<br />soon as we track down your friend, we'll have questions for her too. My best<br />head hunters are looking for her now.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He placed a<br />file on the desk. “Tell me, Col. Ashland, how much do you remember about<br />me?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />are Dr. Briar, we met outside the cafe in Oregon, and you smell like peppermint<br />candy."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes,<br />all true. But how about before that time at the coffee shop, is there anything<br />else you remember about me?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"No.<br />Just what I’ve read about you online. Should there be?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We<br />shall see." Dr Briar pulled out some wires and pads. "While you were<br />out, you were injected with a serum of my own design. It will help you be more<br />forth coming. I'm simply going to attach these to you for monitoring. I promise<br />it won't hurt. We tried putting them on while you were out, but you kept<br />sweating them off...." He placed the pads on Ash's temples, neck, chest,<br />and forearms. "Tell me, Col. Ashland, how long have you been having these<br />nightmares?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Are<br />you my psychologist now?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Let's<br />just say that I am an interested party."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I get<br />bad dreams, left over from the war. Not that it's any of your business."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar<br />continued to press with question after pointless question. Ash found it to be<br />the oddest interrogation he has ever been aware of. Not once did Dr. Briar ask<br />him why he broke in, or what he hoped to accomplish, or what he had done while<br />in the facility. He just kept pressing him with questions about his life,<br />habits, and preferences in food. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It was the<br />kind of small talk Ash always tried to avoid in normal life, and yet he was<br />tied to a chair in here. So he could either answer these pointless questions,<br />or say nothing.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He was<br />pretty sure that nothing was a bad option, because they'd probably put him back<br />away for a while. He would use this opportunity to counter-interrogate. So he<br />kept Dr. Briar talking while he answered the Life Survey as Dr. Briar called<br />it.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">After what<br />felt like hours of incessant questioning, but the clock assured him it was only<br />45 minutes, Dr. Briar placed is pen on top of his notebook, and leaned back<br />into the chair. His eyebrows furrowed, and the skin around his fat nose<br />wrinkled.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash, sat<br />back in his chair. Let the urge to break his own thumbs to escape the handcuffs<br />pass for a moment. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The door<br />was still locked, and he didn't want to risk killing the good Dr. He was most<br />definitely someone who needed to be arrested (given the circumstances), but he<br />wasn't yet sure he needed to die. Not yet anyway. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">******* Ash<br />needs ot have another sighing here.**********<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Post Escape<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Eta makes it back<br />to Chesters. They think they are spying on Dr. Briar, and the tables are<br />turned. The connection is cut. Will Ash be killed?<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Two hours<br />passed since she had left the Tower. On the inside, Eta was feeling the intense<br />pressure to get back to Chester and make a game plan. Her training told her to<br />make sure she wouldn't be followed first. She'd borrowed a car from the Denny's<br />parking lot. She'd driven in the wrong directions for miles, making frequent<br />turns, watching for familiar faces or vehicles... after an hour she was sure<br />she wasn't followed, or found.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">She parked<br />the car at a different Denny's, placed the keys into an envelope with some cash<br />(enough to buy a better car), and put it on the floor board by the pedals. She called<br />an anonymous tip from a payphone. With luck, the Police would have the car<br />before the owner knew it was missing. She'd wiped it down for prints and<br />evidence.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Five<br />minutes later, the Metro Bus took her to the Metro Rail. That was four hours<br />ago, when she arrived at Union Station. She'd walked from there. The car they<br />had rented was in a safe place, it was rented under an assumed name, so the<br />Institute shouldn't be able to find it. They'd have to go back for it when it<br />was safe, unless Ash could get to it without being seen.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">When she<br />walked up to Chester’s place and stepped in, she could hear him typing away. He<br />turned around in relief. "Oh man! I'm so glad you are safe!"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I am,<br />but I think they have Ash. I came back here to re-group and re-gear, then I'm<br />headed back up there."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Actually,<br />Ash wasn't the one I was worried about. You were the one I was worried<br />about..." He paused. His eyes were not on her face. “Um. Nice outfit.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta could<br />feel her skeptical face coming on, she could always feel it in her left<br />eyebrow. She was turning red, heat run up her neck.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester was<br />too busy to notice her reaction, he turned back to the monitor and call her<br />over. “The virus worked. I've tapped into every system they have, and they<br />don't know it's even in there. They have him in a room controlled by electronic<br />locks. These people obviously never considered a real cyber threat after I<br />left. All my backdoors are still in place."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The monitor<br />showed Ash strapped to an expensive looking chair, in the middle of a very<br />white room. His arms and feet restrained with metal cuffs.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Now,<br />I can work miracles with systems, but there is nothing I can do about those<br />cuffs." Chester threw up his hands. "That is all up to him."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Alright."<br />Eta ran up the stairs and back again. She unzipped the duffle bag she had left<br />in her room. Then strapped on a new set of clips for her Glocks. She changed<br />jackets, and looks. "You monitor from here. I'll stand by in case he needs<br />a ride."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Where<br />will you get another ride?" Chester glanced back. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I'll<br />improvise." Eta held up the new phone Chester had prepared while she was<br />changing gear, and waved it at him. Then turned around and headed out the door.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Let Go<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Dr. Briar needs<br />to tie the boston bombings to a series of information security breaches. And<br />point him toward Dark Matter.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash let out<br />a big breath, "So Doctor... Did you run out of questions? I thought we<br />were having fun?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"If I<br />release your hands from those cuffs, would you promise not to try to<br />escape?" The Doctor's eye said he was serious. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash's hands<br />hurt, and he hated to break promises, but it would be nice to get out of the<br />cuffs without breaking his own thumb. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The Dr.<br />stood up, and walked over, releasing the cuffs. "I have just decided,<br />against my better judgement, to trust you Col Ashland." Dr. Briar took a<br />sip of water. He handed Ash a water bottle, and sat back down.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"OK.<br />Thank you, I think. Trust me how?" Offer trust to the captive, good<br />technique. Ash used it himself in Afghanistan.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Please,<br />let me get my thoughts together" Dr. Briar closed his eyes, and bowed his<br />head. He put his hands together and rested his forehead on his two index<br />fingers. "Your friends have attempted a hack of our security system which<br />means that this session is not being recorded for a while as the system is<br />resetting. What I have decided to tell you could cost me dearly if it were to<br />be discovered by my..." The doctor's eyes glanced to the floor<br />"...benefactor."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash decided<br />to go along with whatever this was for the time being. "Dr. Briar, you<br />have my attention."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I was<br />supposed to have you killed today when I got everything I needed from you. Just<br />like I was supposed to have you killed in Oregon. Then, against my better<br />judgment, I sent you those warnings." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"So...<br />The text messages." Ash felt a tingle run up his spine. "That was<br />you?" he sat back in the chair, and blew a sigh.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I am<br />still not sure why. It obviously cost me as you turned around and broke into my<br />lab. I can play this off as the brilliant scientist but incompetent soldier;<br />however, you will need to escape on your own." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Not<br />that I am not grateful, but can you clue me in here? I'm a bit lost." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />think you broke into my lab because my website reminded you of your dreams, and<br />you have been having strange experiences. Well this is half true. The website<br />is designed this way on purpose. It is a calling card to all of our<br />experimentees. When people run searches for the experiences they've had, this<br />site will come up in the top listings." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash sat<br />forward, and stared Dr. Briar in the eye.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Tell<br />me Ash," Dr. Briar stood and began pacing back and forth behind the desk.<br />“Are you familiar with Psychotropic Warfare?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Sure"<br />Ash jumped in. "We used various forms in the field. We would pipe in sound<br />that drove the locals nuts, disperse crowds, and in one instance, the people<br />went crazy and started a riot amongst themselves." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Well<br />you only experienced the tip of the iceberg. Those were the weapons that were<br />tested and released. Many thousands of others are being researched by DARPA,<br />and those funded by people like them. At the turn of the century one of the<br />most brilliant men of his time, Nicola Tesla, performed experiments that<br />revealed air at its ordinary pressure, is a conductor for large amounts of<br />electrical energy. He theorized that electricity could be generated at one<br />location, and transmitted to another without the use of wires." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar<br />took another sip of water, and started talking faster. "Some<br />mathematicians and scientists believe that Tesla's work would provide the basis<br />for understanding seemingly paranormal phenomena like telepathy. At the same<br />time Tesla, who was an engineer and mathematician, and had gained experience in<br />telephony and electrical engineering before immigrating to the United States in<br />1884, was studying the properties of waves as it relates to electricity.<br />Meanwhile, others using his work as a basis for other wave forms started the<br />modern field of acoustics..." Dr. Briar stopped, his eyes searching the<br />blank walls like planes looking for a place to land. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Doctor,<br />you're losing me a bit here." Ash said, “Did you have a point?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I'm<br />sorry" Dr. Briar put his hands up, "I get so excited about the<br />possibilities. Where was I....? Ah yes... Have you heard of Extremely Low<br />Frequency, or ELF?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />got me there," Ash sat back again, "No idea". <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"When<br />researchers started applying Tesla's equations in electricity waves to sound,<br />they found interesting applications. The human ear can generally hear between<br />20 Hertz to 20,000 Hertz. Exceptions outside that range are possible, but rare,<br />and usually found in the young. ELF started as experiments using<br />"sound" frequencies below the level of human hearing. Think of a dog<br />hearing a dog whistle, but you can't. Same idea, but the dog whistle is higher<br />than normal human hearing, and the ELF's are lower. Anyway, when ELF's are<br />produced at high pressure levels, think volume, they have been found to cause<br />interesting physiological results." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Dr.<br />Briar, your enthusiasm is hard to miss, but I'm still missing the point."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes,<br />yes, I'm always so lost in the details, bear with me and I will get to the<br />point, I promise. We do not have much time before the system resets. So,<br />research has shown that external ELF magnetic fields induce electric fields and<br />currents in the body which, at very high field strengths, cause nerve and<br />muscle stimulation and changes in nerve cell excitability in the central<br />nervous system. Due to the ethical issues regarding testing human subjects,<br />experiments on dogs were conducted at levels of 170dB at a frequency of 0.5 Hz.<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Curiously<br />the dogs stopped breathing because of lung ventilation due to the high<br />intensity pressure changes. The 0.5 Hz frequency of the sound acted as an<br />artificial respirator and the dogs showed no ill effects afterwards. Also,<br />psycho-acoustic warfare was used in the Waco siege at the Davidian compound in<br />Texas."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"This<br />is not exactly news, that's what I said before. Like the crowds dispersal units<br />we used in Afghanistan." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes,<br />but, they can do more than just irritate the body and mind. Prolonged exposure<br />to ELF's from normal electronic appliances, like TV's and Smart Phones, have<br />been shown to activate the ATM-Chk2-p21 pathway in HaCaT cells, inhibiting cell<br />proliferation."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash raised<br />an eyebrow.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Meaning,<br />that they change the brain and cell structure itself! The cells were actually<br />prevented from normal replication, and in some cases mutating, as a result of<br />ELF's. On the flip side, there are indications that there could be positive<br />benefits from exposure to certain ELF's and not others. Many of the most<br />profound effects of ELF are attributed to infrasound in the region of 7 hertz.<br />This corresponds with the median alpha-rhythm frequencies of the brain, which<br />predominantly originate from the occipital lobe during wakeful relaxation with<br />closed eyes. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Our<br />research found that frequencies under 7 hertz create a general feeling of<br />relaxation and wellbeing, known as the alpha state. One of the most beneficial<br />frequencies on earth is said to be the 6.8 hertz frequency. Interestingly, the<br />Pyramid at Giza has a constant frequency of 6.8 hertz running through it.<br />However, curiously, this is the resonant frequency of the body's organs and<br />hence organ rupture and death can occur at high intensity exposures to certain<br />frequencies in this range. We had to be very careful." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash<br />motioned to keep going, because he was going to anyway.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Several<br />decades ago I was working as a Higher Research Scientist at the National<br />Physical Laboratory in England. I was in charge of a project to study Optimum<br />Biological Frequency Resonance, or OBFR. The hypothesis was that our biological<br />system is "tuned into" the background frequency of our planet, which<br />is a steady pulse of 7.83 hertz which beats around the planet within the<br />earth-ionosphere cavity. While testing several new acoustic chambers I stumbled<br />across an interesting development in our Negative Sound chamber."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash held up<br />his hand, "I'm sorry, Negative Sound?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Yes,<br />it does 'sound' odd, doesn't it?” the doctor chuckled at his own joke. “The<br />room is located 100's of feet below the surface to avoid ambient noise, and<br />then covered in deep sound baffling wall to wall, floor to ceiling. The subject<br />is asked to sit in a chair in the center of the room. Because any sound you<br />make does not bounce back to you from the wall, as it would in any normal space<br />we live in, the result can feel as though the sound were negative. This simply<br />means that the subject is experiencing less sound than they have ever<br />experienced, not that the sound is actually below zero, that is<br />impossible." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash could<br />feel his gut sink... "Are you telling me that room actually exists?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The Doctor<br />paused, "Yes, why?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"It's<br />from my nightmares, I see that room every night." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Well,<br />that is to be expected. You see, you were one of many volunteers who were<br />subjected to our experiments during Operation White Noise."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Excuse<br />me? Volunteers? I do not recall Volunteering for anything." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"That<br />is unfortunate. I will come to that, but please, allow me a moment to get to<br />that. In due time, in due time..." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The Dr.<br />took another sip of water, and sat down on the edge of the desk in front of<br />Ash. "When we subjected our volunteers to the room, we noticed that some<br />began to hallucinate. Most chalked that up to the vast capacity of the human<br />mind to fill the void with something. However, I had already begun separate<br />experiments in paranormal research and ELF effects. We found that we could<br />place the subject in a clean room first, and bombard them with white noise,<br />which sounds like static. Call it an acoustic palate cleanser before the main<br />meal. Slowly, over a period of time determined by the subject’s readings, we<br />would introduce specific ELF's to determine their effect. We would then move<br />them to the Negative Sound room, and continue only the ELF's from there. They<br />could not hear the ELF's, but they were there being played in “silence”. At<br />that point something amazing happened. The subjects were able to temporarily<br />tear back the veal, and see into the beyond." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"The<br />Beyond?" Ash could feel his jaw tense, "What do you mean,<br />beyond?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Different<br />subjects were exposed to different ELF's, ranging from 5 hertz, the lowest we<br />could produce with our equipment at the time, on up to 15 hertz. We found that<br />6.66 hertz, 7.77 hertz, and 19 hertz had the most profound effects. Subjects<br />exposed to the 19 hertz would experience the 'haunted house' effect, skin<br />crawling, feeling like someone passed them. At 6.66 hertz the subject would<br />actually see dark creatures around them, and in some extreme cases hear them.<br />Others were exposed to the 7.77 hertz range, and they would see light<br />creatures. Some, you for example, were exposed to all three stages several<br />times. Curiously, the exposure was not the only factor. Some responded<br />differently than others."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"OK,<br />I'm hearing you... But none of that explains why your 'benefactor' wants me<br />dead." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Well...<br />The experiments went fine. The test subjects did not seem to have any long term<br />affects and were not able to duplicate the experiences outside the chamber. The<br />only lasting affect was that prolonged exposure seemed to have an effect on<br />short term memory. We believe this may have something to do with a symptom we<br />have called the Eternity Effect."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Eternity<br />Effect?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"The<br />subject loses all sense of time and place, and has to be brought back down to<br />reality before release. This often takes a few more weeks in the clean room.<br />After that they were released, and no worse for the wear. Until..." The<br />Doctor hesitated for the first time in minutes. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Until...<br />What?" Ash prodded.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We<br />could not have known, never have expected..." The Doctor mumbled to<br />himself.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Doctor<br />Briar. What? Until What?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Years<br />later, as subjects came in contact with the naturally occurring ELF's, their<br />symptoms reappeared outside the room, out in real life. The old man, the crazy<br />one you confronted, he was one of ours too. But he was worked on years before<br />you were. The first time we realized this new result was when we were contacted<br />by an emergency room. We were listed as the primary care physician in those<br />days. The man had quit his job in the middle of the shift, and walked out into<br />oncoming traffic on the freeway. When he was asked why, he was told the voices<br />told him to. He was hurt, but they stabilized him. The next day he snuck out of<br />his room, walked into traffic again, and died on impact. The resulting accident<br />caused more fatalities." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"That's<br />terrible..." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We<br />thought he was just a bad egg, pre-disposed to craziness before we got to him.<br />But then it started happening again, and again. Not all of them, mind you, but<br />more than could be statistically explained by pre-disposition. We now believe<br />it has to do with the increase of technology everywhere. There are more ELF's<br />and they build in standing waves in people’s homes, jobs, cars, radio towers,<br />cell towers, the list goes on. It was determined that we were responsible, and<br />we needed to conduct damage control, before anyone outside could be become<br />aware of us. Monitor each subject. Anyone showing symptoms would be put down<br />before they could harm others."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I<br />imagine that you know that is illegal?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Of<br />course, but the tests themselves were cutting edge, and corners were cut."<br /><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“So the<br />tests were illegal too.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“In a<br />manner of speaking. Off the books, certain agencies found the data useful, and<br />looked the other way.” He sighed. “I only learned later that the funding for<br />the project was not entirely above board. We could not tell the authorities<br />about all the subjects. The lawsuits would cripple us. We decided that anyone<br />showing symptoms would be locked away if we could arrange it, but that too risked<br />exposure. Eventually we started putting them down. I only agreed in the most<br />extreme of cases at first, but then once I was in, I could not get out. I could<br />both continue my work and get my hands dirty, or I would be put down too."<br />The doctor turned white at the thought.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash let out<br />a sigh, and wiped his face.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Which<br />brings me to you. I just cannot do it. You are a good man, and I just couldn't<br />do it. Then you had to show up here and ruin my one good deed. But it occurs to<br />me that we may be able to help each other now. There is another volunteer that<br />was showing promise, and then he turned out to be the worst of them all. He is<br />out there in the world causing untold harm and havoc, and he must be stopped.<br />However, despite my best resources, I cannot find him. It's possible that you,<br />with your Special Forces background, could." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Alright,<br />let's just say that I'm buying your story for now. Something still doesn't add<br />up. I was not released from some clean room. I went missing from a top secret<br />training facility, and weeks later I was found on a desert island with no<br />memory. How does that fit in to your volunteer story?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />were special, you and your friend. We needed subjects from specific backgrounds<br />that were not as likely to volunteer. So, we arranged to have them delivered. I<br />cannot tell you more, I wish I could. I was the Chief Scientist, but I did not<br />handle the... Business End. You showed up and I put you into the clean room<br />before you woke. Afterward, you were delivered back to your people. I cannot tell<br />you who, or how...” Doctor Briar leaned closer, "But I can tell you that<br />we could not have done it without help from your own government. You be careful<br />who you trust. I know DARPA funds were siphoned more than once for our<br />project." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash sat,<br />silently. Staring into the floor. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The Doctor<br />walked around to his desk, and pulled open the drawer. The doctor came back<br />around, and dropped a small white envelope into Ash's inside jacket pocket,<br />then put his finger to his mouth. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I<br />don't believe there is anything else I can tell you at the moment. I cannot<br />help you escape from here without looking complicit. The system will be reset<br />shortly. When it does the cameras will reboot. There is an exit down the hall<br />that leads to the outside."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He pressed<br />a button on the wall behind him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“Yes Doctor<br />Briar."&nbsp; A man answered. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We<br />are done in here, there is nothing more I can learn today. Take him to his<br />cell, and we can do this again tomorrow." Dr. Briar smiled and winked,<br />then his eyes turned downward.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Two guards<br />entered the room, and asked him to stand up. They recuffed him, and motioned to<br />the door. As they passed the desk he shoved his weight into one guard knocking<br />him to the ground. The other was blinded by a quick head butt to the bridge of<br />the nose. Ash put his shoulder into the Doctor's chest, and kicked his knee to<br />the side. The Doctor went down to the ground.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"A<br />little too easily" Ash thought, "but hey, this isn't the<br />Oscars". <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He grabbed<br />the keys from the desk and ran down the hall, unlocking his cuffs on the way.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash crashed<br />through the door, and headed off into the night. The system must have<br />eventually reset, because he could hear sirens in the distance, but only after<br />he was a few miles from the facility. With no camera's it was a wild goose<br />chase to know which direction he had headed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Recovered<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta got the<br />call from Chester that Ash had escaped out of the back entrance. One emergency<br />powered camera showed him heading east toward town. Which left only three<br />directions he may have really been headed. She glanced at the Google map of the<br />area, in satellite view. She had been here only last night. But the terrain<br />looked different in the early morning light. North and West were both fields<br />and highways. South led back to the beach. Beaches are easier to get lost in.<br />He could take the conspicuous clothing off without needing to find an entirely<br />new outfit. He may have to grab a pair of shorts. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta drove<br />the newly borrowed Corvette down to the coast. She saw a bum sitting on the<br />bench with what appeared to be a newly acquired military grade jacket. She<br />pulled up to him, and asked where he got it. Then he pointed her toward the<br />beach shop nearby.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Thanks<br />buddy." Eta nodded and pulled up along the curb in front of the shop. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Took<br />you long enough." A voice spoke from behind her. She could feel her heart<br />jump into her throat. How does he do that? <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You<br />could get shot..." She said without turning around. "... For sneaking<br />up on a girl like that."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I<br />could" Ash sat came up and leaned on the window of the convertible she’d<br />‘borrowed’, "but life is worth the risk some times."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"So..."<br />Eta pulled her sun glasses down. He was wearing the same pants from the night<br />before, but he had torn them into cut-off shorts and he managed to acquire a<br />new a new tank top that said “Malibu Style”. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He had put<br />on a few pounds since they worked together in Israel, but he didn't age badly.<br />She cast the thought aside. No. Not him. "Now what grandpa?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Now,<br />we make sure I wasn't followed. Then I take this" Ash pulled a thumb drive<br />out of a white envelope. "to Chester." He slipped it back into the<br />envelope, then his pocket. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“The audio<br />was out thanks to Chester’s virus and the system reboot. But it kept his access<br />to the cameras, even though there’s was down. Chester noticed that he put<br />something into your pocket before you escaped. What’s on it? And why did he<br />give it to you?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“I didn’t<br />exactly escape. The good Doctor Briar let me go… well… I guess he didn’t let me<br />go, as much as he didn’t make it hard to escape. The only thing in here was the<br />thumb drive, and a name on the outside. "Darkmatter". <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“That’s an<br />interesting turn of events.” Eta said. “Why, exactly, would he do that?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“It’s a<br />long story, I’m not sure that I trust him… tell you on the way.” Ash jumped<br />over the door into the passenger seat. “Uh… that is as soon as you tell me<br />where you got a bright blue 2016 Corvette.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta could<br />feel the grin crawl across her face. She couldn’t help it. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;“A bit conspicuous… don’t you think?” Ash<br />turned back to Eta. He had his superior officer face on, but she could see the<br />smile he was fighting back. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“Zillow is<br />amazing for finding wealthy people selling their third homes in this recession.<br />This guy still had his car collection in the garage in one of the photos, and<br />his ‘sophisticated’ key box wasn’t that sophisticated.” She shrugged. “I had to<br />do something with my time while I was waiting on Chester to tell me where to<br />go.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;“Hey” Ash interrupted her moment. Trade spots<br />with me “I’m driving.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“HA!” Eta<br />snorted. “Go steal… er… borrow… your own sports car. I’m driving.” She looked<br />him in the eye. “Besides, we both know that I’m a better driver, and I’d like<br />to return this in one piece so he never knows it was missing.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash tilted<br />his head and looked like he wanted to say something, then threw a glance at his<br />shoulder stitches and put on his seat belt.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Her fingers<br />fumbled for the phone in her Cowboy Holster bag. She paid UrbanTool $115.00 for<br />this glorified fanny pack. But she hated purses, and this was just that cool.<br />She handed a phone to Ash, “This one is yours, call Chester and let him know I<br />found you.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Then she<br />pressed the start button and the engine roared to life.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Thumb Drive<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: He learned some<br />of the secret to their past, in exchange he is let go to hunt down a bigger<br />fish. Dark Matter. Ash escapes with a thumb drive. It contains a list of<br />everything he knows about Dark Matter's operations. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash could feel his<br />stomach gurgling. He couldn’t tell if it was mad at him for starving it so<br />long, or grateful for the new supply. They had brought a large order from Thai<br />Banquet on the way to Chester’s, and they were all sitting around the table<br />digesting the events of the past few days.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash spent most of<br />dinner recapping the conversation with Dr. Briar, while Chester ran a debugging<br />program on a non-network computer. “We’re not about to just open that thing<br />before we know what Trojan horse is on there.” He’d already walked them through<br />making sure that there was no GPS tracker before he let them get close to the<br />city. The video chat came in very handy, he didn’t have to rely on their<br />descriptions.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So… You guys were<br />actually part of these experiments.” It was more of a statement, than a<br />question. Chester turned to Ash, “Why don’t you remember being a part of it?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“If Dr. Briar is<br />to be believed” Ash said, “And there’s no telling yet if he is… The experiments<br />cause the cells of the brain to re-structure, in some cases in dramatic ways. He<br />said that these experiments were forcing the next evolution of mankind.<br />Unfortunately, short-term memory loss is common for most test subjects. The<br />memory loss is worse for some than others.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;“Why would some people experience greater<br />effects than others?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“The ones that<br />experienced the greatest changes to the brain’s molecular structure have more<br />changes in the temporary memory area of the brain? He said that some people<br />were more susceptible to the experiment. He thinks that these are the humans<br />who were genetically pre-disposed toward the next evolution of mankind, more<br />advanced already, and his experiment just pushes them along. Kind of like a<br />jump start?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Huh… We’re like…<br />X-Men?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Something like<br />that. ‘Evolved’ is the word he used.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta glanced down<br />at her Styrofoam bowl. “I don’t feel very evolved.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Me neither.” Ash<br />took another bite of Pad Thai. “He said the dreams are latent memories from the<br />experiment itself. Some memories, those with more powerful emotions are more<br />‘sticky’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The laptop on the<br />far end of the table beeped. “Done.” Chester said, and he went over to the<br />keyboard and started typing. After a few moments of silent chewing and watching<br />from Eta and Ash, Chester stopped typing and turned the monitor to them. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So… what is it?”<br />Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Files.
