Thousands<br />of files. I'm fairly certain that non of this was on the data I hacked from the<br />Tower. This is top secret stuff you wouldn't keep on a connected server. It<br />looks like there is a file for every experiment they ever ran for 20 years.<br />They are grouped into categories. Danger. No Danger. Evaluating. Missing.”<br />Chester pointed to the file names, “Check this out though. Next to each name is<br />an indicator ‘Alive’, ‘Passed’, or ‘Eliminated’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That sounds<br />ominous.” Eta pointed with her chopsticks. “Is my name in there?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Chester’s fingers<br />flew through some files, “Yeppers. You and Ash are both in here. The date says<br />it was about eight years ago. It may take a while to go through these. Most of<br />the file is just charts and graphs.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well… that<br />certainly matches with the time period we got ‘Medically Discharged’. General<br />Akbar was so mad." Eta said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Doing his best<br />impression, Ash stuck out his chest: “‘Two of my best agents went missing for<br />weeks, and can’t even tell me where they were? Of all the asinine bull! You had<br />better come up with something better than that for your actions, or I’ll have<br />you court martialed.’ “Ash chuckled, “He was not happy.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;“That is, until we were subjected to every<br />test known to man, and he was certain we were telling the truth. Then he just<br />looked crushed, like someone took his candy away.” Eta feigned a pout. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well, especially<br />for you.” Ash threw a wadded napkin at her. “You were his poster child. The pilot<br />project. The first woman to be allowed to join the Combat Control. You were<br />‘paving the way for other women’ he said.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yeah, then he<br />followed it with ‘… so don’t screw it up Romanakov.’ <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I’m just glad<br />that I was at Fairchild while you were at Lackland. I heard you gave those boys<br />a hard time.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I did.” Eta’s<br />eyebrows furrowed, “They deserved it too.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“And then when<br />they called us in to the new tactical unit, only 6 chosen in the whole Air<br />Force. He was so mad when he had to discharge us, after all that.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Let’s not pretend<br />he was sentimental. He was just losing his investment. He had to start over<br />with two new recruits, 46 months lost. That’s what really got to him.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash could still<br />feel the heat burning up his neck when he was being questioned by his own<br />commanding officer. How dare he accuse him of lying? Ash was the most dedicated<br />servant the US Air Force had ever had, and he was not going to just sit here<br />while some bureaucrat in a uniform questioned his loyalties. That was what he<br />felt. What he did was what he was trained to do, sit there and take it. Submit<br />to the tests, and prove he was right through the process.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Did you see his<br />face when he had to apologize to us?” Eta laughed.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash remembered the<br />joy it brought him when the General apologized for his outburst. “He’s been<br />atoning for that ever since. I assume he still contacts you twice a year for<br />progress updates?” Ash asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He sure does.<br />Like clockwork. Last time-” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Hey guys…”<br />Chester interrupted. “Did you say General Akbar?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yes, that was our<br />commanding officer. Why?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Would that be<br />General George Akbar, USAF? Commanding Officer of the Air Force Special<br />Operations Command?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yes…” Eta sat<br />forward. He ears pulled back ever so slightly when she was interested. Ash had<br />always found that to be a funny quirk. “Why?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He’s in these<br />files too.” Chester said, his voice tone sounded as confused as Ash felt.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“General Akbar was<br />experimented on too?” Ash asked. He could feel his own heart beating in his<br />chest. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not exactly.”<br />Chester said. “He’s under a separate file labeled ‘Contributors’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The three of them<br />crowded around the screen, pouring over the file. “General Akbar was the one<br />who delivered us to Dr. Briar?” Ash wasn’t sure if Eta sounded mad, confused,<br />both?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Hold on…” Chester<br />said. “There are some intake notes here.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The screen read:<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Colonel Theron "Ash" Ashland,<br />USAF Spec Ops.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Technical Sergeant Nadine Etaine “Eta”<br />Romanakov, USAF Spec Ops.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Contributed as possible use for USAF.<br />DARPA funding under the Enhanced Soldiers initiative. Copy all test results to<br />DARPA and General Akbar.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Wait…” Eta back<br />up into the couch near the table. Her eyes narrowed, and mouth pursed together,<br />“He knew where we were? He knew and still drilled us like that? Of all the-”<br />Eta cut herself off mumbling to herself in Russian.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash wasn’t sure<br />how to feel. Confused, sure. Angry, obviously. Yet, somehow, it felt… right.<br />Like that annoying piece you cannot find on those hidden images games his<br />students were always playing on their phones. They would always get so mad that<br />he could spot it in 10 seconds when they had been looking for hours. But this<br />had been a puzzle he just couldn't piece together until just now.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“It makes sense.”<br />Ash said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta threw him a<br />stare.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No, it makes<br />sense.” He continued. “I always assumed he checked in on us because he was<br />sorry for our strange departure. Or because we were high value assets and he<br />was hoping we’d eventually remember something useful for his investigation.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“OK?” Eta’s eye's<br />told him she was waiting for the punchline.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He was trying to<br />see if we’d remembered something, but not for his investigation. He was trying<br />to see if his investment had paid off. He wanted super-soldiers, and instead he<br />got broken soldiers with memory loss and bad dreams. He’s been hoping something<br />would eventually happen.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That lying horses<br />butt.” Eta sat down on the edge of the couch.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“OK.” Chester<br />piped in. “So… what does that mean for us now? Can we trust this guy? This<br />General? Or how about the Dr. Briar?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“As for Dr. Briar,<br />I think we can all agree he may be useful, but we cannot trust him. As for the<br />General, I was trained to never trust anyone. That’s why I’ve only checked in<br />from random payphones that show a circle around a different neighborhood blocks<br />from here.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You trusted me<br />pretty quickly.” Chester said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well, I didn’t<br />have much choice with you.” Ash replied. “We did not really having a plan when<br />we got here. Coming to your rescue made plans for that night harder.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Why did you come<br />to rescue me?” Chester inquired. “You could have let it go, you didn’t know<br />that I was worth saving.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Didn’t have much<br />choice their either. Your friend was very convincing.” Ash said. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“My friend?”<br />Chester asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yeah, what<br />friend?” Eta joined in.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That night, when<br />I heard the commotion in the alley. I looked around the corner and saw the<br />group harassing you. Then I saw a flash of light. When it cleared I saw a large<br />man, maybe eight feet tall… with a white robe, like Caesar… standing behind<br />you. He looked at you, smiled at me, motioned his hands to the men, and then<br />vanished. I kind of couldn’t help myself. I rushed in without knowing exactly<br />why. How do you turn down a guy like that?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You saw an angel<br />behind me?” Chester seamed more amused than surprised. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I guess that’s<br />what you’d call him. Sure. Fits the description.” Ash said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well how about<br />that.” Chester said. “Good job Herbert.” He gave a high five to the air behind<br />him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Herbert?” Eta<br />asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That’s what I<br />call my Angel.” Chester said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You’ve seen your<br />angel too?” She asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No. But God<br />doesn’t really tell us their names, and we’re not supposed to worship them, or<br />get fixated on them. But I always feel better knowing the people I’m hanging<br />with. So I gave him a name.” Chester said flatly. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You believe in<br />angels huh?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Of course. Don’t<br />you?” Chester asked, his eyes widened. “After all you’ve been through lately,<br />surely the supernatural doesn’t shock you?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I’m still not<br />sure what to believe about all of this. I’ve just been hoping I wasn’t nuts,<br />and for some rational explanation. It sounds like we’re onto one now, with the<br />whole evolution thing. But angels? I mean, next you’re going to tell me you<br />believe in elves and wizards.”&nbsp; <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not such a wild<br />assumption on your part, but magic has always been the layman’s explanation for<br />things science hasn’t understood yet.” Chester pulled his Android out and held<br />it up to them. “Imagine you went back in time to the days of Knights and<br />Inquisitions. You show them a smart phone, and play music from it, or a Ted<br />Talk, or a video of a cat attacking a lighted Christmas tree. You think they<br />would ask you for one? Maybe a few would, but most would want to burn you at<br />the stake for the use of magic.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Point taken” Eta<br />said, “But still… angels?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You think this<br />world is all there is? We are living on one of several dimensions. Angels and<br />Demons are just created beings, like us, that live on another level, impacting<br />ours. Ash merely saw through the veil for a moment.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“And me?” Eta<br />asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Assuming that<br />Einstein was right about time and space, and string theory is right about<br />multiple dimensions, time is a dimension, like height, width, and depth. If you<br />were to jump out of the four dimensional box, you would be outside of time.<br />Past, present, and future, would all be tangible to you at the same time, like<br />the three dimensions of a Rubik’s Cube are available to you now. So ‘future<br />visions’ are nothing more than your ability to operate, partially and<br />temporarily, outside of space-time.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That sounds a bit<br />wild, so how did you come to this conclusion?” Eta seamed still unsure.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Math and Science<br />were pre-requisites for a PhD in Computer Science. This is common topic of<br />interest on the Science Chat Rooms.” Chester shrugged. “Hey, it’s getting late.<br />It could take years to sort through these files manually. Let me run a search<br />algorithm to compare the data in the files for your mystery man<br />"Darkmatter" to the internet news feeds and dark web searches. This<br />may help to bring us to the most relevant information. I used to use that when<br />studying for tests. Came in very handy. Who wants to read worthless stuff, when<br />you can jump to the answers? Then we can sort through that in the morning.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta nodded in<br />agreement, and with a yawn headed off down the hall. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash piled the<br />plates and such on the counter and put the left-overs in the fridge. “Thanks<br />Chester, for everything. I know we’re imposing here.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Are you kidding?”<br />Chester said. “This is the most fun I’ve had in my life. See you tomorrow.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Observation<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar<br />pulled up his monitor at his desk in the tower.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Status Report?<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Subject<br />WildCard took the bait.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Good. Keep<br />watch and monitor results.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He pulled out up the footage of Chester's house. The heat<br />signatures showed three inside. Now they would lead him to the real prize.</span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /><br /></span><br /><br /><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Episode<br />2 - The Parade</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene 6</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Waqas</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The costumed men<br />and women were dancing through the streets. A Snoopy Balloon passed by at her<br />level, 100’s of feet above the crowds. Gift Boxes, the size of houses,<br />followed. She could see dear old Santa at the end, rounding the corner. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">A new element appeared.<br />A young man, holding a remote control. He and his father were wearing Santa<br />hats, and fake white beards. She followed their gaze toward a small Unmanned<br />Arial Vehicle. It was shaped like the Millennium falcon, but she could see<br />cameras attached to it. They were apparently trying to get a better glimpse of<br />the parade. It flew in an out of the balloons. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s when she<br />noticed that they seemed to be avoiding the television cameras, and security<br />stations. Quite expertly in fact. It was odd, these visions, she could see near<br />and far at the same time without obscuring either set of images. The pair were<br />not smiling, like the rest of the crowd. They were not pointing and laughing at<br />the parade floats. The father was whispering in the son’s ear, and the son was<br />intent on following the path of his UAV. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The toy plane made<br />its way up and over the last set of balloons, and then down toward Santa,<br />nearly knocking him off his pedestal. He looked more annoyed than frightened.<br />It swooped back up to the last balloon, and under.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta willed herself<br />to float down for a better look. As it approached the underside, a small<br />package fixed to the vinyl started to emit a red light. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Wait… no. The red<br />light wasn’t coming from the package, it was coming from somewhere else. The<br />UAV. A metal point was on the front end of the UAV and attached to it was a<br />laser pointer. The kind you might find at Dollar Tree. It was flying straight<br />up now, 90 degrees, at full speed. The red dot fixed directly at the middle of<br />the box.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Impact. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She’d come to<br />expect what was next. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">First the flash…<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">…then the<br />percussion against her chest…<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">…then the sound of<br />explosion.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She was hurled<br />through the sky half way across the city. The devastation was enormous. Whole<br />buildings collapsed. People that had not been crushed were running from the<br />scene. But it would do no good. The air was windy, and an orange cloud wafted<br />in all directions. The runners were dropping dead within feet, some within<br />blocks.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Walking, nearly<br />oblivious to the commotion, through the dust were a father and son. Wearing gas<br />masks. They hopped onto a parked motorcycle, on a side street. And headed into<br />the city, and they were gone. She could just make the first three numbers of<br />the Virginia License, K38…<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta could see the<br />kid smile, raise his fingers to the sky, and toss the Santa hat and beard to<br />the wind. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She watched as the<br />costume floated through the air, landing on a body nearby.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta woke. The<br />dream, night after night. She lay in her bed, staring at the ceiling for a few<br />moments. Knowing that sleep would not soon return, she got up and walked to the<br />window. She opened it and let the cool air rush over her. It almost reminded<br />her of home.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She couldn’t get<br />the image of Santa out of her head. She always thought that a white Santa was<br />odd. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Everyone knows<br />that Santa was a dark skinned Russian.” Her Mamochka would start the heated,<br />good natured debate with Da’. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No dear,” Da’<br />would counter. “He was an Irishman, plain as the nose on your face.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">And on the debate<br />would run. Reindeer? Please. Not these brown skinny things in American<br />cartoons. He used large chested white Reindeer, with long pointed antlers, like<br />tree branches with no leaves. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s the Santa<br />her Mamochka taught her about.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Of course, there<br />is no Santa… no elves, no fairies, no Jesus, no Mohamed, no Buddha. All<br />trappings of religious non-sense to keep ignorant people in line, and full of<br />pointless hope. And when those ignorant people got too far out of line, they<br />sent in Eta to put them down. Well… they used to, until she got broken. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So…” She broke<br />the silence. Considering the conversation earlier that night. “I’m not just<br />broken… I’m also a failed experiment. Why doesn’t that surprise me?” Her voice<br />fell deaf on the evening breeze.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Images of her<br />parents, when they were alive. Christmas with family and friends. Like it<br />should be. Presents, not many, too poor. But they would come up with something<br />every Christmas, despite themselves. And then her life changed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“America?” She<br />said one day. “Really? We’re going to America?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">All she’d ever<br />known was the temperate town of Letterkenny, Ireland. Her mother fled to<br />Ireland during World War 2, when the Russians were working loosely with the<br />westerners. She got her chance to leave as a “Volunteer Field Nurse”. She<br />managed to claim asylum in newly formed Republic of Ireland, who had been<br />sitting out the war as a neutral party. The Irish were sympathetic to her<br />cause, as they too were under pressure constant pressure to roll over and<br />accept the so called “United Kingdom”. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s where she<br />met her Irish lover, a man after her own heart. They thought that kids were not<br />in their cards, until she got pregnant in her mid 40’s. Her parents were older<br />than the other kid’s parents, but she didn’t mind. It made her special. At<br />least, that’s what she was told growing up.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">At 10 years old,<br />in 1976, her father, frightened by the escalating violence and the Kingsmill<br />Massacre, decided to leave. He took his wife and daughter to start over in<br />America. That’s when it all fell apart. He worked two jobs, with no family to<br />fall back on, and died of a heart attack before she turned 16. Her mother died<br />a year later of pneumonia, probably compounded by grief. By 18 she was the<br />ridicule of school with the oddest of odd accents, and she didn’t fit anywhere.<br />Then she was left to fend for herself. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not to worry,” a<br />friend told her. “The military takes freaks like you all the time.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">And with a 99 on<br />the ASVAB, she could do anything she wanted. She found a job in intelligence,<br />and worked her way up the ranks. Which led her to today… a total mess.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta brushed off<br />the past, and tried to remember the new elements of her dream.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She could still<br />see the face of the young man with the remote, the father not so much.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">One word popped<br />into her head. It sounded so funny. She said it out loud. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Waqas”<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She rolled it onto<br />her tongue, and repeated it over and over. She found the word instantly<br />amusing, but sad at the same time.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She let the<br />images, of the dream and her childhood Christmas, battle each other for another<br />hour, looking out the window into the night sky. Eventually she felt a yawn<br />come on, stretching the back of her throat. That was a good sign that the dream<br />was wearing off, and she could head back to bed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The covers<br />swallowed her, like an ocean. And she was the island swallowed by it.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Discovery</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Ash and Eta<br />compare the files on dark matter to the dark net search results.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Dr. Briar needs to tie<br />the boston bombings to a series of information security breaches. And point him<br />toward Dark Matter.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">They discover information<br />on Dark Matter, the boston bombings, and the pattern search algoruhtym points<br />them toward a particular terrorist cell in the north east. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">They were<br />gathered around the multi-monitor set of screens, and Chester displayed the<br />most likely candidates for further review. </span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Closet to Rooftop</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Back at Chesters,<br />Ash is resting in the bedroom, when is transported to a man on a roof top, then<br />ends up back in his bedroom. What he learns gives them their next clue. A<br />Terrorist named ..... doesn't want to go through with it. They must find him.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It was the<br />sound. It washed over his mind, like a shower on the inside. Water landing on<br />pavement, concrete, and wood; as though beating a million tiny drums. The<br />distant sound of the tiny river, washing down the sidewalks into the gutters.<br />Ash always slept with the window open, but most especially on rainy nights.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">We are no<br />closer now to getting to the bottom of this, than we were when it started.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The rest of<br />the house was asleep, but he couldn't find sleep. The air shifted slightly, and<br />he felt he wasn't alone. Ash sat up straight, and at the edge of his bed sat a<br />man. He was wearing blue jeans, a T-shirt, and a well-kept beard. His hair was<br />pulled back into a pony tail, and he smiled. The man's eyes held a piercing<br />gaze, frightening and peaceful all at once.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The man<br />stood, and motioned to Ash. Almost unable to fight his own body, he rose to<br />follow the man to the closet door. He reached out to open it, and a blinding<br />light raced across the room. When Ash opened his eyes again, they were not in<br />his room anymore. They were standing on a rooftop, overlooking New York City,<br />and Herald Square.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">There,<br />sitting on the ledge was a young man, maybe no more than 20. He was holding<br />himself, rocking himself back and forth.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It'll all<br />be over soon. It'll all be over soon. He repeated to himself over and over.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash could<br />see the Parade balloons in the distance. He knew this is what Eta had been<br />talking about. Ash wanted to go stop him, but he couldn't move. His traveling<br />companion put his arm on Ash's shoulder and smiled. He could feel the answer.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Not Now.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The bright<br />light flashed, and he found himself standing in front of his open closet,<br />staring at the darkness. Ash turned back to his bed, and lay there for a few<br />more hours. Sleep overcame him, and he rested. For the first time in a long<br />time, confused, but peaceful, he rested.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">First Macy's Vision</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; She's standing in midair, 100 feet or<br />more above the crowds. She can see a line of people in costumes dancing down<br />the street, with large decorated vehicles in between groups. There are crowds<br />on both sides of the street bundled in heavy clothing and a light dusting of<br />snow from earlier today. She doesn't feel the chill, she's a spectator, not a<br />participant. Eta floats, stationary, observing.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Suddenly, her vision is blocked by<br />something brown and right up in her face, she wills herself a few steps back,<br />and a large brown balloon fills her vision. A few more steps back and it's as<br />large as a house. As it passes she can take in a full view. It's a... turkey?<br />Yes, it's a turkey the size of a house, floating high above the crowds, and<br />just below and even with her.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; She can see Santa on his sleigh<br />toward the end of the event... She knows this somehow. It's familiar, something<br />her parents were involved in before she shut out them, their memories, and<br />society in general. What's it called? There's a tradition for this? Santa and<br />his float are just below her now. She sees the clock on the building, it's 1:17<br />pm...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Flash. Her chest is compressed, she<br />can't breathe. The buildings around her crumble. She is thrown back 1,000's of<br />feet. She takes the next breath she sees the cloud, just like old WWII videos.<br />A mushroom cloud. The buildings are evaporating in every direction. The<br />inhabitants won't even know what hit them until it's far, far too late. It's<br />moving with great speed for miles, buildings evaporate before the wave in every<br />direction.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Devastation...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">As she<br />sucks in a deep breath the world around her evaporates and turns black, then<br />white, then she wakes up. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash was white. "You do know what you are saying right?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I guess so. Some parade is about to be attacked?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Not just some parade, Eta. The Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade! That's<br />an icon of Americana. It's filled with innocent people, including lots of<br />children. That would be a disaster beyond the Twin Towers." Ash sat on a<br />stool and sighed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /><br /></span><br /><br /><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Episode<br />3 - Checkmate</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene 2</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Your Move</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Dark Matter<br />escapes. But his base of operations is shut down for now. Dr. Briar is running<br />for his life from the Anonymous Benefactor. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Like<br />Moriarty to Sherlock, Darkmatter is playing a long term game. He has his<br />players separated from each other not just in location, but in construct. His<br />Jihadists don’t' know about his drug sales, etc. He has False ID's upon False<br />ID's. Layers of organization. He currently works out of a hotel that he owns under<br />a shell Corp. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The F.B.I.,<br />working with Ash, raid a hotel, arresting over 100 people. Computers, hard<br />drives, disks, records, all confiscated. The best stuff is on a single computer<br />upstairs; but the disk was wiped clean. Darkmatter is smart, but he's no<br />hacker. The data is corrupted, but not totally lost. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">In the<br />corner of the room, Ash finds a chessboard, with one pawn knocked over. There<br />is a word written on the side of the white piece. Boom! <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash's phone<br />rings; and Darkmatter says: "let the games begin... Nice hat by the way.<br />You should get that stain on your collar to the dry cleaner." Ash runs to<br />the window, but sees nothing. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Post Credits</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Dr. Briar leaves<br />them with a list of other people affected by the experiments. Some of them are<br />known to be dangerous, others have no apparent effect, and others may be<br />struggling like they were. New Story Goal: ????<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /><br /></span><br /><br /><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Un-Used<br />Scenes</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Change this to Ash's<br />POV, and do something with it</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy Elwood - Sr Audio-Medical Equipment Technician. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Goooood… Did you see that? His blood pressure spiked and he stood up. Eye<br />dilated. Something is starting. We’re almost there…” The voice said. Technician<br />Randy Elwood took note of the time and date. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy Elwood. Sr Medical Equipment Technician at Briar Psychiatric Center.<br />Randy has three PHD’s. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • Audio Physics (Science of<br />Sound) - Just because he could never stop dissecting music. Thought it would be<br />fun to study sound. Turned out to be his favorite degree. Though, he wasn’t<br />sure what he could do with it, other than make great You Tube Video<br />Soundtracks, which is how he paid for most of his training and school. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • One in Group Behavior -<br />Maybe he could figure out how to interact with these humans he lived with. He’d<br />never been much good throughout his life.
