Turns out the degree only made him<br />better able to understand and predict human behavior, not mimic it to any<br />realist believable degree. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • Human Anatomy-Biology -<br />Maybe understanding the body better he could understand the mind and social<br />patterns. Not so much. Just made for more awkward conversations are the few<br />parties he tried to attend. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Although incredibly bright, Randy was a perpetual student. His professors<br />advised he leave the comforts of school for the real world. Only so much can be<br />learned in the library. With a few phone calls he was connected to to the Briar<br />Psychiatric Center to assist in the on going study of sound waves on the human<br />mind.&nbsp;&nbsp; <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">He didn’t mind the gig. He spent most of his time in a lab, by himself,<br />reading and watching monitors. Not bad for $100,000 a year job. Especially not<br />bad for the first paying job he’d had outside of school. Real job anyway, he<br />still made a few sound tracks now and then. This was a great gig for someone<br />with his specialty: “Not interacting with other people.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy checked the monitors one last time before returning to his book. It<br />was assigned reading by his new employer. Supposed to help the workers<br />understand the “culture” of the company. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Unlocking the Paranormal Mind. A study of how the human mind’s unlocked<br />potential”, By Dr Richard T. Briar. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Couldn’t be worse than doing nothing, or that whole semester he took wasted<br />reading about the breeding behavior of wombats! <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">*** <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Boston Chaos</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The<br />moonless midnight clung to the walls like a gulf oil spill. The flashes of<br />color and bursts of brightness, reflected off of his glasses. Images of people<br />screaming and running from the bombs. No one even noticed the purses and<br />wallets being snatched. And no one noticed the three banks being hit. But most<br />importantly, no one noticed the override being installed on the network cables<br />underground a few blocks over. Lights on his computers spun to life. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Darkmatter,<br />as he was known these days, got out of his chair and hopped around the room,<br />laughing <i>hysterically</i>! <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"These<br />idiots fall for the Jihad thing every time! It's like a broken record stuck on<br />my favorite song, it never gets old." Darkmatter smiled to his latest<br />conquests. The twins, fraternal brother and sister, were his biggest fans. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">His fingers went<br />to blasting through the data now streaming into his network. He could see<br />everything. From corporate servers, to granny's desktops. He had access to it<br />all. And they wouldn't notice for months! They'll be too busy looking for some<br />rag tag brothers with a death wish. With the install re-buried, under the<br />concrete, it will take a long time for anyone to figure out it's even there, if<br />they ever do. In that amount of time, he'll have data mined millions of<br />computers and servers. He can use this to install a botnet on every user<br />connected to this line, and rotate out from there. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">All it cost<br />was sending some lousy people off the planet early. They weren't doing anything<br />productive anyway. His guides were proud of him, he could tell. They didn't say<br />much, they just stood in the corner. But they were smiling. So he was on the<br />right track. Too bad other's couldn't see Hecate and Adrasteia. Then again, why<br />share their ancient knowledge with mere mortals. He was transcendant. One of<br />the few on earth meant to ascend to higher realms of knowledge and power. The<br />mortals would be gone eventually, so what if he sent a few early. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He enjoyed<br />the chaos that came working with the Jihadists. They were self destructive<br />idiots, but in them he got a two for the price of one deal. He could cause the<br />chaos needed for his empire to run, and take them out of the genetic pool at<br />the same time. Win-Win.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The monitor<br />on the wall buzzed, it was that Dr. Briar again. He was alerted when he was<br />trying to be found online. He set up a program to alert him when it happened,<br />and then send a false lead. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Brilliant."<br />he said. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Excuse<br />me sir." A large security gaurd stood in the doorway. "The fans are<br />chanting for you." <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We<br />mustn't dissapoint the fans. 'Francis, we mustn' keep our fans waiting!' "<br />A young man that could be his brother, if not for the Maryln Manson get up,<br />nodded, and rushed out the door to the party downstairs. It's amazing how much<br />noise 100's of teenagers and music can make, enough to cover up the real<br />operations here. The key to operating an organization like this, it to ensure<br />that you were not recognized as the man in charge. Set up a dummy, like<br />Francis, to take the fall when it goes south. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It will<br />always go south, make contingencies. All the people who turn states evidence<br />will sware that Francis was the king of this castle. And Walter Vanderbuilt,<br />Darkmatter, Bob... Whatever name he was going by these days... Will just walk<br />out the side door and dissappear. That's the beauty of this operation. If it<br />wasn't for that silly Dr. Briar... He'd have no thorns at all.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Darkmatter<br />lay on the couch, Hecate and Adrasteia came to him, massaging his shoulders,<br />and whispering promises of the grand future. He closed his eyes and the<br />darkness over took him... sweet comforting darkness. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Lair</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">As the tablet's glow grew to life, an image appeared. On the image of a<br />full moon, two torches crossed paths at the top, over the scales of justice<br />below. The torches were made of gold, but held black and white flames. Upon the<br />gold scales sat two weights named Adrasteia and Hekate . <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Dark Elf entered his password, and the screens around the room booted to<br />life. As he checked his monitors he was able to observe 1000's of cameras from<br />around the world, but front and center were his prime monitors. From here he<br />could see his entire operation. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Anonymous served it's purpose, now it was time to come out of the shadows.<br />It was amazing how much could be accomplished through some keystrokes and well<br />placed religious bull. Dark Elf roamed the dark net, smirking periodically at<br />Hacker Aliases like "HellRazzer567". Really? That's the best you<br />could come up with. Children. It was time to establish his own ID. Something to<br />set him apart from his Anonymous friends. Dark Elf would do nicely. It was<br />simple enough to remember, innocuous enough to be tracked to 100's of other<br />individuals. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">So far the instructions of his masters had paid dividends many times over.<br />He was rich, powerful, and operated from the shadows. Just levitate a few<br />chairs and the powerless will flock to you as though you had all the answers. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The Islamist Extremists were so easy to manipulate too. It was astonishing<br />how easy it was to get some bearded scarf wearer to bomb a building by hacking<br />their website and posting the challenge. These people were not any smarter than<br />these looser addicts that were constantly doing his bidding at his hotel. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The great Dr. Briar was becoming a nuisance, but he was assured that he<br />still served a purpose. He had a few ideas about what to do when that purpose<br />was complete. Hiring a man to play the part of wealthy contributor? 100,000<br />dollars. The look on his face when he got the texts? Priceless. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">What a moron.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Meanwhile, it was nice to be king. Lord rather. Lord Dark Elf. It had a<br />nice ring to it, eh? <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The best part of living in a hotel was total freedom. It was even better<br />that he owned the hotel, and all the staff were his servants. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The room was dark, he'd been floating so many aliases, that <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Alternate</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">************<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Ash..."<br />Chester spoke into the laptop mic. No response. He was still out cold. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The five<br />entered the room&nbsp; with some fan fare. The<br />lab coat turned to face the camera. It was Dr. Briar.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;"Hello... who ever you are." Dr.<br />Briar smiled.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester and<br />Eta looked at each other in horror.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We've<br />discovered your little virus. And we're quite safe here, I assure you."<br />Dr. Briar turned to Ash and pulled out a siringe. He injected a clear solution<br />directly into his neck. No reaction. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar<br />turned to a computer system on the wall, and pushed a button, and winked at the<br />camera. The monitor went blank. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta and<br />Chester looked at each other, mouths open, a look fear crossed Chester's eyes. <o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta felt a<br />sinking feeling in her stomach. font-size: 12.0pt;">Chester and Ms. Wu's</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">They're<br />here!<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester<br />snapped awake...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">His heart<br />pumped against his chest like a battering ram, with the police just outside the<br />door. Chester's skin was cold, clammy. Bed clothes were soaked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Fog slowly<br />cleared from his mind, under the light of a new dawn...<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">***asdf<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"They<br />felt so real." Chester said to Ms. Wu, as he helped her run his own<br />personal brand of Anti Virus software on the Orphanage Computers. He could have<br />done it from home, but he enjoyed their weekly talks. "Black leather and<br />mist were swirling about me, chasing me through the streets. I bolted into this<br />old church on my right and locked the bolt. They were banging into the door,<br />clambering to get through, the door was creaking... then I woke up."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Interesting..."<br />Ms. Wu said, as she picked up a "Coding Basics for Dummies" book, and<br />placed it on the shelf.<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"It<br />was so real... even though I've been awake for a few hours now, it still feels<br />real."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Let<br />me ask.." turning to him, "Why were you afraid of them?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The<br />question had not occurred to him. "I...Why wouldn't I be? They were scary<br />monsters."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Sure,<br />but they were locked outside, you were safe inside. You escaped them."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"But<br />there were getting in."<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Were<br />they? Or were they trying to get in an failing?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Mr. Wu had<br />a knack for turning things upside down.</span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><br /><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /><br /></div><br />
